name: Italian Sausage Terrine
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook and stir the sausage until brown; drain and reserve.
2. Place the remaining ingredients except bacon in a blender container.
3. Cover and blend on high speed until smooth, about 45 seconds.
4. Stir the blended mixture into the reserved sausage.
5. Line a loaf pan (9 x 5 x 3 inches) with heavy-duty aluminum foil, leaving about 3 inches overhanging sides.
6. Place bacon slices crosswise across the bottom and up the sides of the pan, letting slices overhang the edges of the pan.
7. Pour the sausage mixture into the pan; fold the bacon over the top.
8. Place the loaf pan in a shallow pan and pour very hot water (1 inch) into the shallow pan.
9. Bake uncovered at 350°F for 1 1/2 hours.
10. Remove the loaf pan from the hot water; fold the foil over the top.
11. Place a weight on the terrine (an unopened 46-ounce juice can makes a good weight).
12. Press down firmly for 2 minutes.
13. Leave the weight on the terrine and refrigerate until firm, about 6 hours.
14. To remove the terrine, loosen the foil from the sides of the pan and grasp the ends of the foil, lift out; remove the foil.
15. Cut the terrine into 1/4-inch slices.
Serve with thinly sliced bread if desired.
